sourceMovie.title: Missing
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Missing works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Searching
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Missing clicked for you, Searching (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Missing clicked for you, Inheritance (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Missing?
faq.answer: Try Searching, Inheritance, Panic Room—each shares DNA with Missing while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Missing?
faq.answer: Missing (2023) is best described as Drama, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Missing part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Searching Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
